source.title: Alinna-Dawson-Thin-blood-WotB-Sheet-V5
source.localPath: source-sheets\SPECIAL CHARACTERS - Ask before handing these out.\Alinna-Dawson-Thin-blood-WotB-Sheet-V5.xlsx

folderPath: SPECIAL CHARACTERS - Ask before handing these out.

Print Sheet: Note: with your flaw, you can never raise any discipline higher than Level 4. You can't create blood bonds.
Allies: Friends from college who remember you and took you in and who keep your secret.
UCLA Professor you now work for
Dexterity - (Burst of Speed, Disarm, Fighting Blind, or Quick Draw)
A girl who was in the wrong place at the wrong time, you have come home after years of horror to find everything changed - and a secret you have to keep from everyone.
You know the status of everyone in your sect. Leadership will tell you how they got one of them. Alternative Idenity can let you earn and fake status. Maxium 5 Fleeting w/o merits.
Keeping your head down, learning what you can, and protecting what is important.
7/5/3 traits, 3/2/1 backgrounds, 2/1/1 disciplines, 4/3/3/2/2/2/1/1/1/1 abilities, 30pts freebie, 7pts merits max, 7pts flaws max - All Merit/Flaw/Freebies must be on XP Spends Sheet

Page 107 BNS

Attribute 3xp Each

In-Clan Discipline New Level x3

Regain lost Humanity 10xp Each

Merit = to merit rating

Background New Level x1

Skill New Level x1

OutOfClanNew level x4

Technique 12xp each
If you need to add more cells for character creation. Go to row 18 and add from there to make sure none of the forumulas break.

rows: A girl who was in the wrong place at the wrong time, you have come home after years of horror to find everything changed - and a secret you have to keep from everyone.

You had a happy childhood - once. You grew up in a good, large Catholic family in Pasadena, to very normal parents. Your father owned a construction company, your mother was a teacher at St. Anothony's school, and you were the eldest of a pack of younger siblings. Life was pretty good, at least till high school. Both of your parents were working long hours and relying on you to oversee the younger ones, and being good Catholics, were stict with what you could and couldn't do. So you started doing what all teens do, finding little ways to rebel. Music, clothes, recreational drugs, sure, they were all small things you did, thinking you were so grown up.  You couldn't get out of the house fast enough. You got in at UCLA and packed your things into a car and drove to Westwood, not planning to see your family till Thanksgiving at the earliest. You threw yourself into college, new friends, and a lifestyle you knew your mother would disapprove of, but it was finally, FINALLY your life.

Unfortunately, fate worked out differently. One night, you and your roommates went to a club in Hollywood to listen to a metal band you loved. The lead singer, Hakkon, took notice of you and swept you off your feet. Sure, he lead a dark, crazy, goth/metal lifestyle, but hey, he was into you, and it was nice to have the attention just on you for once. You thought it was actual love. You didn't know that he was binding you to him in order to lead you into a trap. Before you knew it, he had taken you to a woman named Elspeth, who sold you to a monster, Sergei, a horrific vampire who wanted a pure, perfect speciment like you. Your blood carried something in it that was special, unique, and valuable. You were taken away to the Ukraine, where Sergei used your blood and body for all sorts of dark and vile purposes, attempting to call up something or do something. You were never sure what he was trying to breed you for, but the horrific nightmares of what occurred still haunt your dreams.

You hadn't realized it made you a vampire till far later, though you gathered from what little you could make of the conversations you were a "thin-blood", someone so weak in potency you were practically human still. You tested the theory when you finally found a way out to escape. Sunlight still damaged you, but not the way it did Sergei. You made a run for it, fleeing until you found help with a priest who though you'd been kidnapped by a sex ring. It was close enough, and it got you away from Sergei and to safety. Once you were safe, you were able to get away and travel, first to Paris, then to London, where you stopped to get funds enough to somehow get home. You got a job and met a good man there, Ahmed, who didn't mind the scars you carried or the awful story you had to tell. He just loved you, real love, not the fake stuff of Hakkon. For the first time since you were taken, you were cared for and safe.

One night, a year ago, Ahmed didn't come home. You don't know what happened, but you grew scared that Sergei somehow had found you. You couldn't let that happen...not that you now carried your beloved Ahmed's child. Fearful of what that monster would do to it, you ran with what money you had, booking the first flight home you could get. You couldn't go home to Pasadena, your family didn't even know you were alive and now you show up pregnant and a vampire? Instead, you found your old college friends, who have graduated and are living in LA. Thrilled you were alive, they took you in and despite their protests, have agreed not to tell your family just yet. So far, they just think that you ran away with Hakkon and it went south and you've been living in Europe ever since. You've fostered that idea, unwilling to tell them the craziness of the truth. Besides, it's better to keep them in the dark.

Amala, your daughter, arrived several months ago, after a difficult pregnancy and a home birth that nearly killed you. Still, despite all that, you are fiercely protective of your daughter, though raising a child as a vampire isn't easy (and your friends are either guilty for having let you get lost or amazing people for helping you take care of her). You've been studying up on your condition, what little information you can find. You accidentally ran into another creature like yourself, and they told you about the Camarilla, about this society of vampires, and how you needed to "get right with them" in order to protect yourself. Every instinct you have tells you to hide, but you have to think of Amala now. You need to ensure that no one gets curious about you and snoops around. Besides, you need to understand what has happened to you - and maybe find Hakkon and Elspeth. You have a score to settle with the both of them for what they did to you and the way they damned you to that hell hole with Sergei. So you are venturing into the demon's maw, as it were, into this society of vampires to find out how to get your revenge and to see if there is anything you can do to fix the situation you are in. More than that, you are in this for Amala - who you are keeping ABSOLUTELY secret from everyone. None of those monsters can ever, ever know about her. If they find out, they may kill her or worse, try to use her for the same evil purpose Sergei tried to use you for, and you'll be damned if you let anyone get their hands on her.
Your relationship to these people is described. This may mean they do not know you.
Keep Amala's existence absolutely secret! No one can know she exists or that she is yours. From what you gather, her existence is unusual. Find out how she even happened and what that means for her.
Hakkon and Elspeth ruined your life. Find out what you can about them and where they are and why they took you and bring them to justice, if you can.
Your friends have been amazing, but you do miss your family. You've not looked into them since you came back home. Figure out how to approach them and how to bring up Amala to them.
